mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market strength that supports corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are negative on average over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned at or below 0.25%, excess liquidity and forward guidance suppress discount rates and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low nominal policy rates and elevated consumer price inflation erode real returns on safe assets, inducing portfolio rebalancing into equities and lifting the S&P 500 over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL produces a rank correlation with 20-day returns below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling period, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock economic recoveries, a rebound in retail sales signals normalization of consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS surprises coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations during identified recovery periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp 3-month acceleration in industrial production signals reviving manufacturing momentum that is quickly priced into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month change exceeds 2%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in housing starts indicates durable residential investment demand that lowers recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST trend shows a rank correlation below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An annual consumer price inflation rate exceeding 2.5% signals impending margin compression and monetary policy tightening that reduces equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 2.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 6% signals deep labor market slack that crushes aggregate income expectations and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 6% over any 2-year rolling window.
